preamble: Lenition of the velar stop /k/ to a palatal glide is a significant stage in many sound changes, particularly in the evolution of Romance languages where /k/ followed by front vowels or in certain clusters palatalizes.

examples.note: The Latin cluster /kt/ developed into /jt/ in Old Spanish (still preserved in Portuguese 'feito') before evolving into the affricate /tʃ/ in 'hecho'.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
